questions.prompt: How do you pass data from a parent component to a child component?
questions.explanation: Props are the standard mechanism for parent-to-child data flow in React.

questions.prompt: Props are mutable — child components can modify the props they receive.
questions.explanation: Props are read-only. To respond to changes, use state and pass new props from the parent.

questions.prompt: What special prop represents the nested elements between a component's opening and closing tags?
questions.explanation: The `children` prop automatically receives any nested JSX.
